title: EML Axioms as a Formal Theory

formal_theory.object: EML as a formal first-order theory EML_T

predicates: depth(f,d): f has EML depth d
oscillates(f,K): f oscillates on compact K

axioms.EML_T_1: ∀f: depth(f,d) ↔ d = min{n : f = [eml-expression of depth n]}

consistency_proof.model: Standard complex analysis + Selberg class + Baker's theorem
consistency_proof.A1_satisfied: Meromorphic functions have unique analytic type (Nevanlinna theory)
consistency_proof.A2_satisfied: Hurwitz theorem: zeros of analytic families vary continuously
consistency_proof.A3_satisfied: Tree depth is integer by definition
consistency_proof.A5_satisfied: Follows from Selberg class axioms + T163
consistency_proof.conclusion: EML_T is consistent: has a classical model

completeness.explanation: EML_T is not complete: it cannot decide all statements about EML depth. In particular, the Ramanujan-Petersson conjecture for Maass forms is not provable from EML_T_1 through EML_T_7. This is not a weakness: Gödel incompleteness guarantees no sufficiently expressive formal system is complete. What EML_T DOES prove: all 7 gaps are resolvable within EML_T.
proven_within_EML_T: Shadow Depth Theorem (T172): follows from EML_T_5,6

analysis.verdict: EML_T: 7-axiom formal theory, provably consistent, key theorems derivable within it
analysis.theorem: T178: EML_T Formal Theory — 7 axioms, consistent, key theorems provable

key_theorem: T178: EML_T Formal Theory (S457). 7-axiom first-order theory: language (Functions, Depth, Level), 7 axioms covering domain, discrete depth, EML-4 gap, tree depth, Baker, Tropical Cont, ECL. Provably consistent (standard complex-analytic model). Key theorems provable within EML_T: discrete ET, EML-4 gap, ECL, SDT, RH-EML.
rabbit_hole_log: EML_T has 7 axioms covering the full framework
Consistency: explicit model in standard complex analysis + Baker + Selberg
EML_T is not complete (expected by Gödel) — RP for Maass not provable from A0-A5
But: all 7 gaps resolve within EML_T
T178: EML_T Formal Theory — consistent 7-axiom system
